news.title: TED, ETS and Khan launch new $10k AI degree
news.summary_en: ETS, TED and the Khan Academy have jointly launched a new AI degree model at a fraction of traditional college costs, vowing to create a globally accessible education pathway backed by global corporate giants.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Europe looks beyond the region in global student recruitment push
news.summary_en: European countries are increasingly looking beyond their borders to recruit international students, signalling a shift in higher education strategy towards global competition, a report by the European Association for International Education (EAIE) has found.
news.author: Beth Kennedy

news.title: Nearly half of South Asian applicants refused Australian study visas
news.summary_en: Nepal and India hardest hit as Australia’s January-March visa data shows tightening, with the sector flagging a lack of clarity amid more stable outcomes for applicants from China and Southeast Asia
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: US: Replacing duration of status set to cause chaos
news.summary_en: The Trump administration’s proposed rule to replace ‘duration of status’ with fixed time limits on student and visitor visas is set to cause administrative chaos, leaders warn, anticipating the change in the coming weeks.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: UK Lords press ministers over sector visa challenges
news.summary_en: The UK minister of state for the Home Office has agreed to meet with sector leaders after concerns about incoming compliance rules and visa refusal rates were raised in the House of Lords.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: France hikes non-EU tuition fees, move dubbed “blow to university autonomy”
news.summary_en: France will require most non-EU students to pay higher tuition fees from 2026/27, as part of a wider push to reshape international recruitment under its 'Choose France for Higher Education' strategy.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Dubai moves back to in-person teaching amid Iran war
news.summary_en: The private education sector in Dubai has taken steps towards resuming onsite education, after moving online amid regional conflict stemming from the Iran war.
news.author: Beth Kennedy

news.title: “Shift beyond big four evident” as Indian student demand holds up amid reset
news.summary_en: With higher education outbound numbers down over a third in two years, consultancies say a “clearly visible” shift sees Indian students look beyond traditional destinations as the market becomes more cautious.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: US politics drives international student self-censorship
news.summary_en: Nearly nine in 10 international students have reported being less willing to speak up in academic spaces or on social media about immigration, global conflict or US regulations, as experts warn of increasingly polarised classrooms.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Through the amber spyglass: a refraction of the new RAG rating
news.summary_en: Alex Lock and Mike Strong consider the unintended consequences of an amber rating from the UKVI's Basic Compliance Assessment (BCA) and how it should not be viewed as failure by universities.
news.author: Alex Lock and Mike Strong

news.title: Relief for some OPT applicants in “immigration limbo”
news.summary_en: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to lift an indefinite freeze on work permit authorisations, including OPT, for a group of international students from travel ban countries.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Canada’s language sector looks to joint pathways to rebuild trust and growth
news.summary_en: Canada’s language education sector has been hit hard by a combination of pandemic disruption, policy change and closures, but a new Joint Pathway Program is emerging as a focal point for renewal.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Scholarship targets study abroad gap for US community colleges
news.summary_en: A new scholarship has launched to expand access to study abroad opportunities to community college students in the US – a population starkly underrepresented in global learning initiatives.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: How many international students are studying in Australia?
news.summary_en: The PIE compiles the most recent data on the number of international students in Australia, including those in the post-graduate work stream, along with insights into the leading source markets.
news.author: Charlotte Greenstein
